title: arenaria capillaris subsp. formosa
intro: Arenaria capillaris subsp. formosa, also known as the small-flowered sandwort, is a plant species that belongs to the carnation family and is primarily found in mountainous regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Arenaria capillaris subsp. formosa has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
